response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional software craftsmanship with 16+ years of development experience, this role requires native-level Japanese proficiency (not met) and specialized expertise in embedded networking security, Linux kernel internals, and low-level C systems programming. The candidate's background is primarily modern web/backend development (TypeScript, Node.js, cloud infrastructure) rather than embedded firmware, security technologies, or network switch development. The fundamental domain gaps and missing language requirement make this a poor fit despite strong general engineering capabilities.
strengths: Demonstrates deep software craftsmanship with consistent focus on TDD, testing frameworks, and CI/CD—practices critical for security-critical embedded systems
16+ years of diverse software development experience across multiple languages (C, C++, Java, D) with proven ability to learn new technologies and domains
Extensive experience with Linux systems, DevOps infrastructure, and complex microservices indicates capability to work with sophisticated technical systems
concerns: Missing native-level Japanese language proficiency, a hard requirement for this role; resume lists only English, Romanian, and basic German
No demonstrated expertise in security technologies (encryption, authentication, attack detection), embedded networking (Layer 2/3, switches, SNMP, NETCONF), or Linux kernel internals
15+ years of web/backend focus (TypeScript, Node.js, AWS); most recent C experience was 10+ years ago at National Instruments in IDE software rather than embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded security and networking expertise
Limited recent C systems and kernel programming experience
